And the clans of the children of Qehath, the Lĕwites, the rest of the children of Qehath, even they had the cities of their lot from the tribe of Ephrayim.
And they gave them Sheḵem with its open land in the mountains of Ephrayim as a city of refuge for the slayer and Gezer with its open land,
and Qiḇtsayim with its open land and Bĕyth Ḥoron with its open land – four cities.
And from the tribe of Dan, Alteqĕh with its open land, Gibbethon with its open land,
Ayalon with its open land and Gath Rimmon with its open land – four cities.
And from the half-tribe of Menashsheh, Ta‛naḵ with its open land and Gath Rimmon with its open land – two cities.
All the ten cities with their open lands were for the rest of the clans of the children of Qehath.

And to the children of Gĕreshon, of the clans of the Lĕwites, from the other halftribe of Menashsheh, they gave Golan in Bashan with its open land as a city of refuge for the slayer and Be‛eshterah with its open land – two cities.
And from the tribe of Yissasḵar, Qishyon with its open land, Daḇerath with its open land,
Yarmuth with its open land and Ĕn Gannim with its open land – four cities.
And from the tribe of Ashĕr, Mish’al with its open land, Aḇdon with its open land,
Ḥelqath with its open land and Reḥoḇ with its open land – four cities.
And from the tribe of Naphtali, Qeḏesh in Galil with its open land as a city of refuge for the slayer and Qartan with its open land – three cities.
All the cities of the Gĕreshonites for their clans were thirteen cities with their open land.
And to the clans of the children of Merari, the rest of the Lĕwites, from the tribe of Zeḇulun, Yoqne‛am with its open land, Qartah with its open land,
Dimnah with its open land, Nahalal with its open land – four cities.
And from the tribe of Re’uḇĕn, Betser with its open land, Yahtsah with its open land,
Qeḏĕmoth with its open land and Mĕpha‛ath with its open land – four cities.
And from the tribe of Gaḏ, Ramoth in Gil‛aḏ with its open land as a city of refuge for the slayer and Maḥanayim with its open land,
Ḥeshbon with its open land, Ya‛zĕr with its open land – four cities in all.
All the cities for the children of Merari for their clans, the rest of the clans of the Lĕwites, were by their lot twelve cities.
All the cities of the Lĕwites within the possession of the children of Yasharal were forty-eight cities with their open lands.
Each of these cities had its open land surrounding it. So it was with all these cities.
Thus HWHY gave to Yasharal all the land of which He had sworn to give to their fathers and they took possession of it and dwelt in it.
And HWHY gave them rest all around, according to all that He had sworn to their fathers. And not a man of all their enemies stood against them, HWHY gave all their enemies into their hand.
Not a word failed of any good word which HWHY had spoken to the house of Yasharal – all came in.
Then Yahusha called for the Re’uḇĕnites and the Gaḏites and half the tribe of Menashsheh,
and said to them, “You, you have guarded all that Mosheh the servant of HWHY commanded you and have obeyed my voice in all that I commanded you.
“You have not left your brothers these many days, up to this day, but have guarded the Charge, the command of HWHY your Aluahim.

“And now HWHY your Aluahim has given rest to your brothers, as He promised them. So now, return and go to your tents and to the land of your possession, which Mosheh the servant of HWHY gave you beyond the Yardĕn.

“Only, diligently guard to do the command and the Torah which Mosheh the servant of HWHY commanded you, to love HWHY your Aluahim and to walk in all His ways and to guard His commands and to cling to Him and to serve Him with all your heart and with all your being.”
And Yahusha baruk them and sent them away and they went to their tents.

And to half the tribe of Menashsheh Mosheh had given a possession in Bashan, but to the other half of it Yahusha gave land among their brothers beyond the Yardĕn, westward. So when Yahusha sent them away to their tents, he also baruk them,

and spoke to them, saying, “Return to your tents with great riches and with very much livestock and with silver and with gold and with bronze and with iron and with countless garments. Divide the spoil of your enemies with your brothers.”

And the children of Re’uḇĕn and the children of Gaḏ and half the tribe of Menashsheh returned and left the children of Yasharal at Shiloh, which is in the land of Kena‛an, to go to the land of Gil‛aḏ, to the land of their possession, which they possessed according to the command of HWHY by the hand of Mosheh.

And they came to the districts of the Yardĕn which is in the land of Kena‛an and the children of Re’uḇĕn and the children of Gaḏ and half the tribe of Menashsheh built an mizbe'ach there by the Yardĕn, a great conspicuous mizbe'ach.

And the children of Yasharal heard it said, “See, the children of Re’uḇĕn and the children of Gaḏ and half the tribe of Menashsheh have built an mizbe'ach opposite the land of Kena‛an, in the districts of the Yardĕn, at the border of the children of Yasharal.”
And when the children of Yasharal heard, all the qahal of the children of Yasharal assembled at Shiloh to go to fight against them.
So the children of Yasharal sent Pineḥas son of Al‛azar the kohen to the children of Re’uḇĕn and to the children of Gaḏ and to half the tribe of Menashsheh, into the land of Gil‛aḏ,
and ten rulers with him, one ruler each from the chief house of every tribe of Yasharal. And each one was the head of the house of his father among the divisions of Yasharal.
And they came to the children of Re’uḇĕn and to the children of Gaḏ and to half the tribe of Menashsheh, to the land of Gil‛aḏ and spoke with them, saying,

“Thus says all the qahal of HWHY, ‘What is this trespass you have committed against the Aluahim of Yasharal, to turn away this day from following HWHY, in that you have built for yourselves an mizbe'ach, to rebel against HWHY this day?
‘Is the crookedness of Pe‛or not enough for us, from which we have not been cleansed til this day, although there was a plague in the qahal of HWHY,
that you turn away today from following HWHY? And it shall be, if you rebel today against HWHY, that tomorrow He is wroth with all the qahal of Yasharal.

‘And indeed, if the land of your possession is unclean, then pass over to the land of the possession of HWHY, where the Dwelling Place of HWHY dwells and take possession among us. But do not rebel against HWHY, nor rebel against us, by building yourselves an mizbe'ach besides the mizbe'ach of HWHY our Aluahim.
‘Did not Aḵan son of Zeraḥ commit a trespass in that which is under the ban and wrath fell on all the qahal of Yasharal? And he was not the only one to die in his crookedness.’ ”
Then the children of Re’uḇĕn and the children of Gaḏ and half the tribe of Menashsheh answered and said to the heads of the divisions of Yasharal,
“Al Aluahim HWHY, Al Aluahim HWHY, He knows and let Yasharal itself know. If this has been in rebellion or if in trespass against HWHY, do not save us this day.
“If we have built ourselves an mizbe'ach to turn from following HWHY or if to offer burnt offerings or grain offerings on it or if to make shalom offerings on it, let HWHY Himself require it.
“But truly, from fear, for a reason we did this, saying, ‘In time to come your sons might speak to our sons, saying, “What have you to do with HWHY Aluahim of Yasharal?
“For HWHY has made the Yardĕn a border between you and us, you children of Re’uḇĕn and children of Gaḏ. You have no portion with HWHY.” So your sons would make our sons stop fearing HWHY.’
“So we said, ‘Let us now prepare to build ourselves an mizbe'ach, not for burnt offering nor for slaughtering,

but to be a witness between you and us and our generations after us, to do the service of HWHY before Him with our burnt offerings, with our slaughterings and with our shalom offerings, that your sons should not say to our sons in time to come, “You have no portion with HWHY.” ’

“So we said that it shall be, when they say this to us or to our generations in time to come, that we shall say, ‘See the pattern of the mizbe'ach of HWHY which our fathers made, though not for burnt offerings nor for slaughterings, but it is a witness between you and us.’

“Far be it from us to rebel against HWHY and to turn from following HWHY this day, to build an mizbe'ach for burnt offerings, for grain offerings, or for slaughterings, besides the mizbe'ach of HWHY our Aluahim which is before His Dwelling Place.”

And Pineḥas the kohen and the rulers of the qahal and the heads of the thousands of Yasharal who were with him, heard the words that the children of Re’uḇĕn and the children of Gaḏ and the children of Menashsheh spoke and it was good in their eyes.

And Pineḥas son of Al‛azar the kohen said to the children of Re’uḇĕn and to the children of Gaḏ and to the children of Menashsheh, “Today we know that HWHY is in our midst, because you have not committed against HWHY this trespass. Now you have delivered the children of Yasharal out of the hand of HWHY.”

And Pineḥas son of Al‛azar the kohen and the rulers, returned from the children of Re’uḇĕn and the children of Gaḏ, from the land of Gil‛aḏ to the land of Kena‛an, to the children of Yasharal and brought back word to them.

So the matter was good in the eyes of the children of Yasharal and the children of Yasharal baruk Aluahim. And they spoke no more of going against to fight, to destroy the land where the children of Re’uḇĕn and Gaḏ dwelt.
And the children of Re’uḇĕn and the children of Gaḏ called the mizbe'ach, “A witness between us that HWHY is Aluahim.”
And it came to be, a long time after HWHY had given rest to Yasharal from all their enemies round about, that Yahusha was old, advanced in years.
And Yahusha called for all Yasharal and for their Alders and for their heads and for their judges and for their officers and said to them, “I am old, advanced in years.
“And you, you have seen all that HWHY your Aluahim has done to all these nations because of you, for HWHY your Aluahim is He who was fighting for you.

“See, I have divided to you by lot these nations that remain, to be an inheritance for your tribes, from the Yardĕn, with all the nations that I have cut off, as far as the Great Sea toward the setting of the sun.
“And HWHY your Aluahim thrust them out from before you and shall drive them out from before you and you shall possess their land, as HWHY your Aluahim has promised you.
“And you shall be very strong to guard and to do all that is written in the Book of the Torah of Mosheh, so as not to turn aside from it right or left,
so as not to go in among these nations, these who remain among you. And make no mention of the name of their mighty ones, nor swear by them, nor serve them nor bow down to them.
“But cling to HWHY your Aluahim, as you have done to this day.
“For HWHY has driven out from before you great and strong nations. As for you, no one has stood against you to this day.
“One man of you put a thousand to flight, for HWHY your Aluahim is He who is fighting for you, as He has promised you.
“And you shall diligently guard yourselves, to love HWHY your Aluahim.
“But if you do turn back at all and cling to the remnant of these nations, these that remain among you and intermarry with them and go in to them and they to you,

know for certain that HWHY your Aluahim shall no longer drive these nations out from before you. And they shall be snares and traps to you and a whip on your sides and thorns in your eyes, until you perish from this good land which HWHY your Aluahim has given you.

“And see, this day I am going the way of all the earth. And you know in all your hearts and in all your beings that not one word has failed of all the good words which HWHY your Aluahim spoke concerning you. All of it has come to you and not one word of them has failed.

“And it shall be, that as every good word has come upon you which HWHY your Aluahim promised you, so HWHY does bring upon you every evil word, until He has destroyed you from this good land which HWHY your Aluahim has given you,

when you transgress the berith of HWHY your Aluahim, which He commanded you and shall go and serve other mighty ones and bow down to them. And the displeasure of HWHY shall burn against you and you shall perish quickly from the good land which He has given you.”

And Yahusha gathered all the tribes of Yasharal to Sheḵem and called for the Alders of Yasharal and for their heads and for their judges and for their officers. And they presented themselves before Aluahim.

And Yahusha said to all the people, “Thus said HWHY Aluahim of Yasharal, ‘Long ago your fathers dwelt beyond the River – Teraḥ, the father of Aḇrahim and the father of Naḥor. And they served other mighty ones.
‘So I took your father Aḇrahim from beyond the River and caused him to go through all the land of Kena‛an and multiplied his offspring and gave him Yitsḥaq.
‘And to Yitsḥaq I gave Ya‛aqoḇ and Ĕsaw. And to Ĕsaw I gave the mountains of Sĕ‛ir to possess, but Ya‛aqoḇ and his children went down to Mitsrayim.
‘Then I sent Mosheh and Aharon and plagued Mitsrayim, as I did in their midst. And afterward I brought you out.
‘And I brought your fathers out of Mitsrayim and you came to the sea. And the Mitsrites pursued your fathers with chariots and horsemen to the Sea of Reeds.

‘And they cried out to HWHY and He put thick darkness between you and the Mitsrites and brought the sea upon them and covered them. And your eyes saw what I did in Mitsrayim. And you dwelt in the wilderness a long time.

‘And I brought you into the land of the Amorites, who dwelt beyond the Yardĕn and they fought with you. But I gave them into your hand and you possessed their land and I destroyed them from before you.
‘And Balaq son of Tsippor, sovereign of Mo’aḇ, rose up and fought against Yasharal and sent and called for Bil‛am son of Be‛or to curse you.
‘But I refused to listen to Bil‛am, so he continued to bless you and I delivered you out of his hand.

‘And you passed over the Yardĕn and came to Yeriḥo. And the masters of Yeriḥo, the Amorite and the Perizzite and the Kena‛anite and the Ḥittite and the Girgashite and the Ḥiwwite and the Yeḇusite fought against you. But I gave them into your hand.
‘And I sent the hornet before you which drove them out from before you, also the two sovereigns of the Amorites, but not with your sword or with your bow.
‘And I gave you a land for which you did not labour and cities which you did not build. And you dwell in them, you eat of the vineyards and olive-trees which you did not plant.’
“And now, fear HWHY, serve Him in perfection and in truth and put away the mighty ones which your fathers served beyond the River and in Mitsrayim and serve HWHY!

“And if it seems evil in your eyes to serve HWHY, choose for yourselves this day whom you are going to serve, whether the mighty ones which your fathers served that were beyond the River, or the mighty ones of the Amorites, in whose land you dwell. But I and my house, we serve HWHY.”
And the people answered and said, “Far be it from us to forsake HWHY, to serve other mighty ones,

for HWHY our Aluahim is He who has brought us and our fathers up out of the land of Mitsrayim, from the house of bondage, who did those great signs before our eyes and has guarded us in all the way that we went and among all the people through whom we passed.
“And HWHY drove out from before us all the people, even the Amorites who dwelt in the land. We too serve HWHY, for He is our Aluahim.”
Then Yahusha said to the people, “You are not able to serve HWHY, for He is a set-apart Aluahim, a jealous Al is He. He does not bear with your transgression and with your sins,
if you forsake HWHY and shall serve mighty ones of a stranger. Then He shall turn back and do you evil and consume you, after He has been good to you.”
And the people said to Yahusha,“No, but we do serve HWHY!”
Then Yahusha said to the people, “You are witnesses against yourselves that you have chosen HWHY for yourselves, to serve Him.” And they said, “Witnesses!”
“And now, put away the mighty ones of the stranger which are in your midst and incline your heart to HWHY Aluahim of Yasharal.”
And the people said to Yahusha, “HWHY our Aluahim we serve and His voice we obey.”
And Yahusha made a berith with the people that day and laid on them a law and a right-ruling in Sheḵem.
Then Yahusha wrote these words in the Book of the Torah of Aluahim. And he took a large stone and set it up there under the oak that was by the set-apart place of HWHY.

And Yahusha said to all the people, “See, this stone is a witness to us, for it has heard all the words of HWHY which He spoke to us. And it shall be a witness against you, lest you lie against your Aluahim.”
Then Yahusha sent the people away, each to his own inheritance.
And after these events it came to be that Yahusha son of Nun, the servant of HWHY, died, one hundred and ten years old.
And they buried him within the border of his inheritance at Timnath Seraḥ, which is in the mountains of Ephrayim, on the north side of Mount Ga‛ash.
And Yasharal served HWHY all the days of Yahusha and all the days of the Alders who outlived Yahusha, who had known all the works of HWHY which He had done for Yasharal.

And the bones of Yosĕph, which the children of Yasharal had brought up out of Mitsrayim, they buried at Sheḵem, in the plot of ground which Ya‛aqoḇ had bought from the sons of Ḥamor the father of Sheḵem for one hundred pieces of silver and which had become an inheritance of the children of Yosĕph.
And Al‛azar son of Aharon died and they buried him in a hill that belonged to Pineḥas his son, which was given to him in the hill country of Ephrayim.
